objects.name: Achinsk Refinery
objects.notes: Eastern Siberia; outside reported drone strike range as of mid-2026.

objects.name: Afipsky Refinery
objects.notes: Struck repeatedly by drones in 2025-2026 (fire at gas/condensate processing unit reported); current operating level unclear.

objects.name: Angarsk Petrochemical Company Refinery
objects.notes: Eastern Siberia; outside reported drone strike range, no credible damage reports as of mid-2026.

objects.name: Antipinsky Refinery (Tyumen)
objects.notes: Design capacity ~9 Mt/yr (Wikipedia registry cites 7.5 Mt/yr, 20% apart; design value used; nominal 180,000 bbl/d). Bankrupt 2019; Sberbank/SOCAR-linked Sokar Energoresurs interim control 2019-2021 (SOCAR held only a brief 9.6% minority and withdrew); sold at bankruptcy auction in 2021 to Rusinvest LLC (since renamed RI-Invest). No credible damage reports as of mid-2026.

objects.name: Anzhersk Refinery
objects.operator: OOO Anzherskaya Neftegazovaya Kompaniya (ANGK), consolidated under AO NefteKhimService
objects.notes: Commissioned 2010; being integrated with the Yaya refinery complex per Russian Wikipedia references; coordinates are city-level for Anzhero-Sudzhensk (approximate).

objects.name: Astrakhan Gas Processing Plant
objects.notes: Gas condensate processing (~3 Mt/yr liquids, approximate) producing gasoline/diesel. Struck by drones in Feb 2025 with months-long processing suspension; mid-2026 status unclear. Coordinates from OSM industrial site.

objects.name: Ilsky Refinery
objects.notes: Set ablaze by drone strike on 2 June 2026; repeatedly targeted since 2023. Coordinates from OSM/Nominatim.

objects.name: Khabarovsk Refinery
objects.owner: independent (NNK - Independent Petroleum and Gas Company)
objects.notes: Russian Far East; outside reported drone strike range as of mid-2026.

objects.name: Kirishi Refinery (KINEF)
objects.notes: Coordinates exact per GEM. Damaged in drone strike March 2026; halted operations after another strike in early May 2026.

objects.name: Komsomolsk Refinery
objects.notes: Russian Far East; outside reported drone strike range as of mid-2026.

objects.name: LUKOIL-Korobkovsky Gas Processing Plant
objects.commodity: associated petroleum gas → LPG / gas condensate
objects.notes: Gas processing plant (not a crude refinery) in Kotovo, Kotovsky district; largest associated-petroleum-gas processor in Russia's Southern Federal District (~450 Mm³/yr). Coords ~2.7 km NW of Kotovo town (satellite-derived).

objects.name: Krasnoarmeysk Oil Pumping Station (LPDS)
objects.notes: Transneft trunk-pipeline pumping station with storage. coords approximate (city/district-level).

objects.name: Krasnodar Refinery (Krasnodareconeft)
objects.owner: Safmar Group (Gutseriev family); branch of Afipsky Refinery LLC since 2021
objects.notes: Struck by drones in 2024-2025; current operating level unclear. Coordinates from OSM plant polygon.

objects.name: Kuibyshev Refinery (Samara)
objects.notes: Reported burning after mass drone attack in early June 2026; repeatedly struck through 2025-2026.

objects.name: Lobkovo Oil Pumping Station (LPDS)
objects.notes: Transneft LPDS pumping station, Moscow-ring oil-products pipeline (same operation/system as Vtorovo). Coords approximate (facility-level).

objects.name: Mari Refinery (Tabashino)
objects.owner: OOO Volzhskie Nefteprodukty (100%); in bankruptcy receivership since Oct 2024; previously Dolgovoy Tsentr structure linked to Moscow Credit Bank (private, not state)
objects.notes: Small independent refinery; struck by drones in 2024-2025; current status unclear.

objects.name: Mariupol Seaport (fuel storage)
objects.notes: Occupied military-logistics seaport; fuel/lubricant storage section. coords approximate (city/district-level). Same op damaged the sanctioned shadow-fleet vessel Lady Augusta.

objects.name: Moscow Refinery (Kapotnya)
objects.notes: Shut down on 17 May 2026 after drone strikes. Coordinates from OSM industrial polygon.

objects.name: Nizhnevartovsk Oil Refining Association
objects.notes: Commissioned 1998; coordinates are city-level for Nizhnevartovsk (approximate, no dedicated Wikipedia article).

objects.name: Nizhny Novgorod Refinery (Kstovo, NORSI)
objects.notes: Struck by drones on 18 May 2026 (fire on site); among central-Russia refineries forced to halt or cut output. Supplies ~30% of Moscow Oblast gasoline.

objects.name: Nizhnekamskneftekhim (NKNK)
objects.notes: Largest synthetic rubber and plastics producer in Russia and one of the largest in Eastern Europe; ~20,000 employees. March 31 2026 industrial explosion (equipment malfunction, 3 dead, 60+ injured) is NOT an attack. Struck by Ukrainian drones in May 2026 (exact date unconfirmed beyond 'as recently as May') and again overnight June 12 2026 with large fire and thick black smoke visible across Nizhnekamsk, 1,200+ km from front line.

objects.name: Novoil Refinery (Novo-Ufimsky)
objects.notes: Primary distillation unit reported burning after repeat drone strike on 2 April 2026. Coordinates from OSM industrial polygon.

objects.name: Novokuibyshevsk Refinery
objects.notes: Primary oil processing halted since 18 April 2026 after drone attack.

objects.name: Novoshakhtinsk Refinery (NZNP)
objects.operator: Novoshakhtinsk Plant of Petroleum Products LLC
objects.notes: Repeatedly struck since 2022; halted crude intake in late December 2025 after drone strikes, with multi-week fires earlier in 2025.

objects.name: Omsk Refinery
objects.notes: Largest refinery in Russia; coordinates exact per GEM. No credible reports of strike damage as of mid-2026 (out of routine drone range).

objects.name: Omsky Kauchuk
objects.notes: Largest petrochemical plant in Omsk Oblast; produces synthetic rubbers, MTBE (high-octane fuel additive), isopropanol; part of Titan Group. New polyisobutylene plant under construction 2024-2025. No confirmed drone strikes in open sources - location ~2,200 km from Ukraine border makes strikes extremely unlikely within current campaign parameters. Coords approximate (city-level).

objects.name: Orsk Refinery (Orsknefteorgsintez)
objects.notes: Struck by drones overnight 29 April 2026; designed to process 6.6 Mt/yr.

objects.name: Perm Refinery (Permnefteorgsintez)
objects.notes: Coordinates exact per GEM. Drone strike (April-May 2026) reported to have halted the refinery deep in the Urals.

objects.name: Ryazan Refinery (RNPK)
objects.notes: Coordinates exact per GEM. Repeatedly struck by drones; suspended operations completely on 15 May 2026.

objects.name: Gazprom Neftekhim Salavat Refinery
objects.notes: Integrated refining/petrochemical complex; hit by drones in 2025 but reported operating normally afterwards. GEM covers it as Gazprom Neftekhim Salavat Chemical Complex.

objects.name: Saratov Refinery
objects.notes: Completely halted operations after drone attack, reported 3 June 2026; repeatedly struck since 2024. Coordinates from OSM industrial polygon.

objects.name: Slavyansk ECO Refinery (Slavyansk-on-Kuban)
objects.notes: Nominal capacity ~5.2 Mt/yr. One of the most frequently struck refineries (2024-2026, incl. Nov 2025 and Jan 2026 hits); operating at reduced level, current status unclear. Coordinates from OSM plant polygon. Capacity discrepancy between sweeps: 5.2 Mt/yr nominal vs 3.99 Mt/yr (registry); nominal value used.

objects.name: Surgut Condensate Stabilization Plant (Chernomyrdin ZSK)
objects.name_local: Сургутский завод стабилизации конденсата им. В.С. Черномырдина
objects.notes: Gas condensate stabilization/processing plant (capacity estimates 8-12 Mt/yr); coordinates exact per GEM. No credible damage reports as of mid-2026. Capacity discrepancy between sweeps: ~8 Mt/yr (GEM) vs 4 Mt/yr (ru-wiki registry table); GEM value used.

objects.name: Syzran Refinery
objects.notes: Halted operations after Ukrainian drone strike on 21 May 2026 (confirmed by Ukraine's General Staff). Capacity sources differ: 8.9 Mt/yr (ru-wiki design) vs 8.5 Mt/yr (Rosneft official crude distillation capacity) vs lower en-wiki list figure; Rosneft official 8.5 used.

objects.name: TAIF-NK Refinery (Nizhnekamsk)
objects.notes: Crude and gas condensate processing ~8 Mt/yr. Nizhnekamsk hub attacked by drones in 2024-2025; no confirmed mid-2026 shutdown.

objects.name: TANECO Refinery (Nizhnekamsk)
objects.notes: GEM lists approximate location 55.6376/51.8197; ru-Wikipedia plant coordinate used. Struck by drones in 2024-2025 but no reported shutdown as of mid-2026. Capacity discrepancy between sweeps: GEM post-expansion ~15.3 vs Wikipedia registry 8.7 Mt/yr (pre-expansion figure); 15.3 used.

objects.name: Tuapse Refinery
objects.notes: Struck four times in April-May 2026; operations suspended indefinitely, tank farm fires and 2026 Tuapse environmental disaster. Capacity discrepancy between sweeps: 12.0 Mt/yr design (GEM/en-wiki) vs 9.03 Mt/yr (ru-wiki registry); design value used.

objects.name: Ufa Refinery (UNPZ)
objects.notes: Coordinates from OSM industrial polygon. Part of Ufa cluster targeted by drones since 2024; no confirmed mid-2026 shutdown report found.

objects.name: Ufaneftekhim Refinery
objects.notes: Coordinates exact per GEM. Ufa refinery cluster repeatedly struck by long-range drones in 2024-2026; current operating level unclear.

objects.name: Ufaorgsintez
objects.notes: Russia's only producer of EPDM rubber; produces ~1/3 of Russia's phenol and acetone, 15% of HDPE; key raw material supplier to three major Russian explosives plants (Sverdlov, Biysk, Cheboksary) with >94 billion rubles in state defense contracts. Struck March 3 2025 (large-scale fire, two explosions, ~1,400 km from Ukraine, SBU-attributed by Ukrainian officials) and October 15 2025 (smoke reported, second strike). Coords approximate (city-level north Ufa industrial zone).

objects.name: Ukhta Refinery
objects.notes: Small northern Lukoil refinery (~4-4.2 Mt/yr); throughput scaled down in 2020s with periodic idling. Struck by Ukrainian drones on 12 Feb 2026 in a record-range (~2,000 km) attack causing fire at the AVT primary distillation and visbreaking units; subsequent operating level unclear. Coordinates from OSM plant polygon.

objects.name: Usinsk Refinery
objects.notes: Commissioned 2011; at the 1.0 Mt/yr threshold; coordinates are city-level for Usinsk (approximate).

objects.name: Ust-Luga Gas Condensate Processing Complex (Novatek)
objects.name_local: Комплекс НОВАТЭК по переработке газового конденсата в Усть-Луге
objects.notes: Gas condensate splitter (3 units x 3 Mt/yr) producing naphtha/jet/fuel oil. Processing and export loadings suspended after major drone-strike fire 24-25 March 2026; later reported operating at roughly half capacity. Merged with the depot/terminal-sweep record for the same Novatek complex (integrated sea export terminal at Ust-Luga port). Coordinates set to the gas condensate fractionation/transshipment complex polygon in the northern part of Ust-Luga port (OSM); earlier sweep point was ~6 km south at the Yug-2/SIBUR area.

objects.name: Volgograd Refinery
objects.notes: Halted operations after 2026 drone attack that stopped CDU-1 (~40% of plant capacity) plus units 5 and 6. Coordinates from OSM industrial polygon / en-Wikipedia.

objects.name: Voronezhsintezkauchuk
objects.notes: SIBUR 100%-owned synthetic rubber plant; capacity ~381 kt/yr; produces SBR, Nd-BR (neodymium butadiene rubber), thermoplastic elastomers - critical for Russian tire industry. New n-butyllithium catalyst plant completed early 2026. No confirmed direct drone strike on the plant itself found in open sources; Voronezh TPP-1 (which supplies plant's heat) was struck November 2025 causing city-wide power disruptions.

objects.name: Yaisky Refinery (Kuzbass)
objects.notes: Western Siberia; outside reported drone strike range as of mid-2026.

objects.name: Yaroslavl Refinery (Slavneft-YANOS)
objects.notes: Coordinates exact per GEM. Hit by confirmed drone strike late April 2026; among refineries forced to halt or cut output in May 2026.

objects.name: Arctic Gate Terminal (Novy Port)
objects.notes: Offshore single-point-mooring loading terminal in the Gulf of Ob at Cape Kamenny; ships Novy Port (Novoportovskoye field) crude year-round via Northern Sea Route; capacity up to 8.5 Mt/yr. Coordinates approximate (SPM ~3.5 km offshore).

objects.name: CPC Marine Terminal (Yuzhnaya Ozereyevka)
objects.notes: SPM offshore loading terminal west of Novorossiysk, terminus of the CPC pipeline; ~63 Mt shipped in 2024 (capacity ~83 Mt/yr after debottlenecking), predominantly Kazakhstani crude. Feeding infrastructure (Kropotkinskaya PS, Kavkazskaya) hit by drones in 2025.

objects.name: De-Kastri Oil Terminal
objects.notes: Export outlet for Sakhalin-1 Sokol crude; annual capacity ~12 Mt (~88 mln bbl); two 100,000 m3 storage tanks. Operatorship transferred from Exxon Neftegas to Rosneft-controlled entity in 2022.

objects.name: Feodosia Marine Oil Terminal
objects.notes: Largest oil transshipment complex in occupied Crimea (rail-to-sea, up to 12 Mt/yr; can store ~250,000 t of fuel); supplies Russian Black Sea Fleet and ground forces. Repeatedly struck (Oct 2024, 2025, 7 June 2026) with multi-day tank fires.

objects.name: Kozmino Oil Terminal
objects.notes: Pacific terminus of the ESPO pipeline near Nakhodka; ESPO blend crude to Asia (~1 mln bbl/d). Design capacity 30 Mt/yr; actual 46 Mt shipped in 2024, Transneft targeting 50 Mt from 2025.

objects.name: Murmansk / Kola Bay STS Transshipment Hub
objects.name_local: Рейдовый перегрузочный комплекс в Кольском заливе (Мурманск)
objects.operator: Multiple (Gazprom Neft FSO Umba; Lukoil-chartered FSU Kola/Lotta)
objects.notes: Ship-to-ship reloading hub for Arctic crude (Varandey, Novy Port, Prirazlomnoye) at Kola Bay anchorages near Murmansk, using floating storage units Umba (Gazprom Neft) and Kola — renamed Lotta — for Lukoil's Varandey crude; key node for shadow-fleet exports, described as Russia's main Arctic oil export hub. (Novatek's LNG FSU Saam operates separately in Ura Bay, ~50 km NW.) In Apr 2026 the Lotta FSU disappeared from AIS, spoofing its position to the Gulf of Guinea. Coordinates are an approximate anchorage location.

objects.name: Petersburg Oil Terminal (St. Petersburg)
objects.notes: Fuel/products export terminal in the Big Port of St. Petersburg: 31 tanks totaling 324,000 m3, annual capacity ~12.5 Mt. Struck by long-range drones 2-3 June 2026 on the eve of SPIEF; storage tank destroyed, large fires.

objects.name: Port Kavkaz Oil and Rail Ferry Terminal
objects.operator: Multiple stevedores: OOO Yugneftekhimtranzit and ZAO Evro-Trans (oil products), OAO Anroskrym (rail/auto ferry complexes), Lada-Gelendzhik-Trans, EvroTEK-Universal
objects.region: Krasnodar Krai, Russia (Chushka Spit, Kerch Strait)
objects.notes: Russia's 5th-largest port by cargo turnover (23.0 Mt 2024; 21.4 Mt 2023), 2nd in Azov-Black Sea basin. Dual role: rail-to-sea ferry hub (Kerch Strait crossing supplying occupied Crimea/Kherson/Zaporizhzhia; rail-ferry lines to Varna, Zonguldak, Poti) and oil-products transshipment (fuel oil, naphtha; ~110,000 b/d fuel oil/residue shipments per S&P Global), incl. roadstead STS transfers. Repeatedly struck: 31 May 2024 naphtha terminal/oil depot (Neptune missiles); 23 Jul 2024 rail ferry Slavyanin damaged, fuel transshipment cut; 22 Aug 2024 rail ferry with fuel wagons sunk; 14 Mar 2026 oil depot and berthing complex hit by drones; 5-6 Apr 2026 Slavyanin (last Kerch rail ferry) destroyed. Port partially operational but oil/ferry infrastructure repeatedly damaged.

objects.name: Prigorodnoye Oil Export Terminal (Sakhalin-2)
objects.notes: Oil export terminal within the Prigorodnoye production complex (Aniva Bay) alongside the Sakhalin-2 LNG plant; remote tanker loading unit 5 km offshore; two 100,000 m3 floating-roof crude tanks; exports Sakhalin Blend.

objects.name: Primorsk Oil Terminal
objects.notes: Russia's largest crude export terminal, terminus of Baltic Pipeline System (BPS-1, 76.5 Mt/yr). Design: crude up to 75 Mt/yr plus ~20 Mt/yr diesel. Hit by drones Sept 2025 and March 2026; Reuters reported loss of at least 40% of tank capacity after the strikes.

objects.name: Sheskharis Oil Terminal (Novorossiysk)
objects.notes: Largest Russian Black Sea crude/products export terminal; endpoint of Transneft trunk lines (Western Siberia, Kazakhstan, Azerbaijan crude). Throughput figure is design capacity (actual ~30-45 Mt/yr); storage ~1.4 mln m3 across the Sheskharis+Grushovaya hub. Struck by drones/naval drones May-June 2026 (tank fires, tanker hit nearby).

objects.name: SIBUR-Kstovo
objects.notes: Produces ethylene, propylene, benzene and hydrocarbon fractions; Ukrainian reporting indicates it also manufactures components used in explosives. SIBUR confirmed temporary operations suspension after drone strike. Located adjacent to Lukoil-Nizhegorodnefteorgsintez refinery in Kstovo industrial zone, ~800 km from Ukraine. Struck November 4 2025 (major fire) and November 24 2025 (10+ explosions reported in industrial zone).

objects.name: Taman Bulk Cargo / Oil Products Terminal (OTEKO)
objects.notes: Black Sea deep-water terminal at Volna (Taman Peninsula) transshipping fuel oil, vacuum gasoil and LPG from rail; used as fallback outlet when Novorossiysk/Tuapse loadings disrupted in 2026. Was attacked by drones in 2024 (LPG tank fires).

objects.name: Temryuk Seaport (Maktren-Nafta terminal)
objects.notes: Azov-coast universal transshipment complex (UN/LOCODE RUTMR): LPG/oil-products/grain/coal/metal; Maktren-Nafta LPG terminal; assessed as a fuel route for Russian logistics to occupied territories. Coords approximate (port entrance).

objects.name: Tuapse Oil Terminal
objects.notes: One of the biggest Black Sea oil terminals, integrated with the 12 Mt/yr Rosneft Tuapse refinery; terminal capacity ~71 mln bbl/yr (~9.7 Mt), mostly refined products. Repeated drone strikes; on 16 Apr 2026 strikes ignited major fires at refinery+terminal (at least 3 killed, benzene/xylene releases, Black Sea oil slick); further strikes 20/28 Apr and May 2026 kept operations suspended.

objects.name: Ust-Labinsk Oil Depot (Poltavskaya Neftebaza)
objects.notes: Fuel storage/distribution (28 tanks, ~14,950 m³); SBU-assessed rear fuel hub for Russian forces.

objects.name: Ust-Luga Crude Oil Terminal
objects.notes: Terminus of Baltic Pipeline System-2 (BTS-2); crude design capacity ~30 Mt/yr (expandable to 38). Port also ships ~33 Mt/yr of oil products. Hit in Ukraine's largest overnight drone attack of 2026 (25 Mar 2026); loadings suspended, then resumed at reduced rates.

objects.name: Varandey Oil Terminal
objects.notes: Fixed offshore ice-resistant offloading terminal (FOIROT) ~21-22 km off the Barents Sea coast; world's northernmost year-round oil terminal (Guinness record); exports Timan-Pechora crude via shuttle tankers to Kola Bay FSUs. Coordinates approximate (offshore platform).

objects.name: Vtorovo Oil Pumping Station (LPDS)
objects.notes: Transneft pipeline pumping station (LPDS) on the Moscow-ring oil-products pipeline; pumps diesel toward Moscow / Baltic export.

objects.name: Vysotsk Oil Products Terminal (Lukoil)
objects.notes: Baltic petroleum-products export terminal on Vysotsky Island, Gulf of Finland (design 11.6 Mt/yr per Lukoil, tank farm 460,000 m3, rail-fed; commissioned 2004-2005); considered as an alternative outlet during 2026 disruption of Primorsk/Ust-Luga.

objects.name: Andreapol Oil Pumping Station (BTS-2)
objects.notes: Pumping station with tankage on BTS-2 feeding the Ust-Luga export terminal. Drone strikes 29 Jan and 13 Feb 2025 damaged filtration/pumping area and additive tanks, forcing temporary shutdown of the trunk line. Coordinates approximate (town-level).

objects.name: Atlas Oil Depot (Rostov Oblast)
objects.operator: FGKU Kombinat Atlas of Rosrezerv (Molodezhny settlement)
objects.notes: Rosrezerv petroleum products depot (FGKU Kombinat Atlas) at Molodezhny settlement, Kamensky district near Kamensk-Shakhtinsky, supplying Russian forces; struck repeatedly (3 and 28 Aug 2024, 28-29 Nov 2024) with multi-tank fires confirmed by satellite imagery. Coordinates settlement-level (Molodezhny).

objects.name: Grushovaya Tank Farm (Novorossiysk)
objects.notes: Main accumulation tank farm of the Sheskharis transshipment hub, in Grushovaya Balka beyond the Markotkh Ridge (~12 km from Novorossiysk, linked by 3.3 km pipeline tunnel); ~40 tanks, 1.28 mln m3, 213 ha. Set ablaze by drone strike 8 June 2026. Coordinates approximate.

objects.name: Kavkazskaya Oil Transshipment Complex (Kropotkin)
objects.operator: Naftatrans rail terminal (Getmanovskaya station); 15.7 km link to CPC Kropotkinskaya PS
objects.notes: Rail-to-pipeline transshipment hub (up to 6 Mt/yr) near Kropotkin with the Naftatrans depot (5 tanks x 20,000 m3, held ~100,000 t at time of attack); connects to CPC Kropotkinskaya PS. Drone strikes 19-21 Mar 2025 destroyed tanks and pipeline; CPC declared pumping via Kavkazskaya impossible for the foreseeable future. Coordinates approximate (stanitsa-level).

objects.name: Kazanorgsintez
objects.notes: Russia's largest polyethylene producer; produces over 170 chemical products including 70%+ from ethylene polymers used in defense applications. Struck January 14 2025: drone wreckage ignited liquefied gas storage tanks on plant premises; multiple tanks caught fire. Authorities claimed minimal damage but three regional airports temporarily closed.

objects.name: Klintsy Oil Depot (Bryansk Oblast)
objects.notes: Regional fuel storage depot near the Ukrainian/Belarusian border; large tank fire after drone attack on 19 Jan 2024 (also struck May 2023), 32 residents evacuated. Coordinates approximate (town-level).

objects.name: Krasny Yar LPDS (Volgograd Oblast)
objects.notes: Linear production dispatch station on the Kuibyshev-Tikhoretsk trunk crude line serving the Volgograd refinery and the Sheskharis export terminal. Drone strike and fire 8 June 2026. Coordinates approximate (settlement-level).

objects.name: Kristall Fuel Depot (Engels)
objects.notes: Strategic aviation fuel storage supplying the Engels-2 strategic bomber airbase (Tu-95/Tu-160). Struck 8 and 13-14 Jan 2025 (fire burned ~5 days) and again 6 June 2026 (satellite imagery showed four tanks burning). Coordinates approximate.

objects.name: Lazarevo LPDS (Kirov Oblast)
objects.notes: Trunk-pipeline LPDS (in service since 1981) with ~50,000 t tank farm in s. Lazarevo, Urzhumsky district, pumping West Siberian crude on the Surgut-Polotsk and Kholmogory-Klin trunk lines (toward central Russia, Belarus/Polotsk and Baltic ports); hit by long-range drones (1,000+ km strike) on 31 May 2026, tank fire confirmed by governor.

objects.name: Nikolskoye Oil Pumping Station (Tambov Oblast)
objects.notes: Major Druzhba trunk station; reported annual station capacity 104.5 Mt of oil/products and daily throughput up to 300,000 t (multi-line node, figure as published by Militarnyi). Drone strike 18 Aug 2025 halted Druzhba pumping; later restored. Coordinates approximate (settlement-level).

objects.name: Kavkaz oil depot (Proletarsk)
objects.owner: Rosrezerv (Russian Federal Agency for State Reserves)
objects.notes: State fuel-reserve tank farm: ~74 tanks x 5,000 m3 (some sources cite 78 tanks) storing diesel, gasoline, aviation fuel and lubricants. Struck by Ukrainian drones on 2024-08-18 and again 2024-08-23; resulting fire burned 16 days and was extinguished 2024-09-02. OSINT satellite analysis: at least 14 tanks destroyed, 4 partially damaged. No public reports of restoration as of mid-2026.

objects.name: Samara LPDS / Oil Logistics Hub
objects.notes: Head blending/dispatch hub of the Druzhba and Kuibyshev-Tikhoretsk systems where Urals blend is formed; largest tank farm in Europe (71 tanks, >1.6 mln m3, 216 ha). Located at Prosvet settlement, Volzhsky district, Samara Oblast. Struck by SSO drones 20 Sep 2025 (confirmed by Ukraine's General Staff) and again in the April 2026 strike campaign. Coordinates settlement-level (Prosvet); distinct from nearby LPDS Lopatino.

objects.name: Stalnoy Kon LPDS (Oryol Oblast)
objects.notes: One of Russia's largest oil-product LPDS (MNPP Ryazan-Tula-Oryol, Transneft-Druzhba/ex Yugo-Zapad Transnefteprodukt), ~170 km from the Ukrainian border; supports product supplies incl. toward Baltic export. Hit multiple times (Nov 2022, Dec 2024 twice with ~12-hour fire, Mar 2025). Coordinates settlement-level: poselok Stalnoy Kon, Orlovsky district, just north of Oryol.

objects.name: Stavrolen
objects.notes: Major Lukoil petrochemical subsidiary; produces polyethylene, polypropylene, benzene, butylene-butadiene fraction. Ukraine's General Staff states plant manufactures drone components, composite materials, seals and insulation for Russian military equipment. Struck October 29 2025, November 12 2025 (SSO confirmed 'successful fire attack'), and December 22-23 2025 (large fire, second strike in six weeks).

objects.name: Sterlitamak Petrochemical Plant (SNKhZ)
objects.commodity: synthetic rubber (isoprene, SBR), aviation fuel additives, antioxidants
objects.notes: Only Russian manufacturer of neodymium synthetic rubbers; produces isoprene and butadiene-styrene rubbers for tires, agidol/ionol aviation fuel additives, and synthetic polymers for defense industrial base. Struck November 4 2025 (water-treatment workshop partial collapse, three separate fire spots, DIU); November 6 2025 (agidol workshop fire, DIU confirmed); April 15 2026 (Liutyi drones, fire, one worker killed, 1,200+ km range).

objects.name: Tikhoretsk Oil Hub / LPDS (Tikhoretskaya)
objects.notes: One of the largest oil hubs in southern Russia, junction of Kuibyshev-Tikhoretsk and Tikhoretsk-Novorossiysk trunk lines feeding the Sheskharis export terminal; large tank farm. Struck by drones 12 Mar and 18 Apr 2026 with tank fires. Coordinates approximate (town-level).

objects.name: Tolyattikauchuk
objects.notes: Supplies ~25% of Russia's synthetic rubber market; butyl rubber critical for tire and military equipment seals. Struck multiple times: April 4 2026 (BK-4 workshop hit, isobutane-isobutylene production disrupted, likely production halt); June 12 2026 (fire reported overnight, confirmed alongside NKNK strikes in same wave, 1,200+ km from front).

objects.name: Unecha (Vysokoye) Oil Pumping Station / LPDS
objects.notes: Key Druzhba pipeline node (capacity ~60 Mt/yr) at Vysokoye, Unechsky district, ~60 km from the Ukrainian border; routes crude to Druzhba (Belarus/EU) and to Ust-Luga via BTS-2. Struck repeatedly in Aug 2025 (two pumping stations wrecked per satellite imagery), later restored. Coordinates approximate (settlement-level).

objects.name: Baku-Novorossiysk Pipeline (Northern Route)
objects.notes: ~1,330 km from the Sangachal Terminal near Baku along the Caspian coast through Dagestan (Makhachkala), bypassing Chechnya via Khasavyurt-Mozdok-Tikhoretsk corridor to the Sheskharis terminal at Novorossiysk. Design ~5 Mt/yr (105,000 b/d); actual flows ~1-2 Mt/yr, partly reverse/swap use. Simplified route.

objects.name: Baltic Pipeline System-1 (BPS-1, to Primorsk)
objects.notes: Trunk route Yaroslavl-Kirishi-Primorsk feeding the Primorsk export terminal on the Gulf of Finland; capacity ~74 Mt/yr after 2006 expansion. Main outlet for Urals crude exports by shadow-fleet tankers. Simplified route via Babaevo/Kirishi corridor.

objects.name: Baltic Pipeline System-2 (BPS-2, Unecha-Ust-Luga)
objects.notes: 998 km from the Unecha junction of Druzhba to the Ust-Luga export terminal; commissioned 2012; ~30 Mt/yr first stage (expandable to 38), plus 172 km branch to Kirishi refinery (branch not modelled). Diverts Druzhba volumes to Baltic tanker exports. Simplified route via Velikiye Luki and Kingisepp.

objects.name: Caspian Pipeline Consortium (CPC) Pipeline (Tengiz-Novorossiysk)
objects.owner: Caspian Pipeline Consortium (Transneft 24%, KazMunayGas 19%, Chevron, LUKOIL, ExxonMobil et al.)
objects.notes: 1,511 km Tengiz-Atyrau-Komsomolskaya-Kropotkin to the CPC Marine Terminal at Yuzhnaya Ozereyevka near Novorossiysk; capacity after the bottleneck-elimination program ~72.5 Mt/yr of Kazakh-origin oil and up to 83 Mt/yr total via Russia; 2024 shipments 63.01 Mt, ~55 Mt Kazakh-origin CPC Blend (not sanctioned). Kropotkinskaya pump station damaged by drone attack Feb 2025, since repaired. Simplified route.

objects.name: Druzhba Pipeline (Northern Branch)
objects.notes: Almetyevsk-Samara-Unecha-Mozyr, then via Brest to Adamowo (PL), Plock and Schwedt (DE). Design capacity of northern branch ~49.8 Mt/yr; Russian crude deliveries to Germany/Poland halted 2022-23, line now carries mainly Kazakh transit crude to Schwedt. Simplified route via published waypoints, tens-of-km accuracy.

objects.name: Druzhba Pipeline (Southern Branch)
objects.owner: Transneft (RU); Gomeltransneft Druzhba (BY); UkrTransNafta (UA); Transpetrol (SK); MOL (HU)
objects.notes: Branches at Mozyr, crosses Ukraine via Brody and the Carpathians to Slovakia (Budkovce-Bratislava/Slovnaft) and Hungary (Fenyeslitke-Tiszaujvaros-Szazhalombatta). ~17 Mt/yr design; still supplies sanctioned-exempt crude to Hungary/Slovakia; flows repeatedly interrupted in 2024-25 by strikes on Russian pumping stations (e.g. Unecha). Simplified route.

objects.name: ESPO-1 Oil Pipeline (Taishet-Skovorodino)
objects.name_local: Восточная Сибирь — Тихий океан, ВСТО-1 (Тайшет — Сковородино)
objects.notes: First stage of ESPO, 2,694 km Taishet-Ust-Kut-Lensk-Olyokminsk-Aldan-Skovorodino, in service since Dec 2009, expanded to 80 Mt/yr design. Routed north of Lake Baikal. Carries ESPO-blend crude for Asian exports. Simplified route via published pump-station towns.

objects.name: ESPO-2 Oil Pipeline (Skovorodino-Kozmino)
objects.name_local: Восточная Сибирь — Тихий океан, ВСТО-2 (Сковородино — Козьмино)
objects.notes: Second stage of ESPO, ~2,046 km from Skovorodino via Belogorsk, Birobidzhan, Khabarovsk and Ussuriysk to the Kozmino export terminal near Nakhodka; commissioned Dec 2012; expanded to 50 Mt/yr. Sole pipeline supply to Kozmino, the main ESPO-blend loading port. Simplified route along the Trans-Siberian corridor.

objects.name: ESPO China Spur (Skovorodino-Mohe-Daqing)
objects.notes: Spur from ESPO at Skovorodino crossing the Amur near Mohe, then twin lines through Heilongjiang to Daqing. Initial 15 Mt/yr (2011), doubled to 30 Mt/yr with second line (2018) under Rosneft-CNPC supply contracts. Simplified route.

objects.name: Kuibyshev–Tikhoretsk Trunk Oil Pipeline
objects.region: Samara Oblast / Saratov Oblast / Volgograd Oblast / Rostov Oblast / Krasnodar Krai, Russia
objects.notes: 1,282 km trunk crude line (820/1020 mm, in service 1974) from Samara/Kuibyshev hub to Tikhoretsk, Krasnodar Krai; main feed for Sheskharis export terminal at Novorossiysk via Tikhoretsk-Novorossiysk 1-3 lines, bypassing Ukraine. Base capacity 35 mtpa, raised to 40 mtpa on Samara-Zenzevatka section in 2022. Joined by Sukhodolnaya-Rodionovskaya link (209 km, 2001). LPDS/NPS along it struck repeatedly by Ukrainian drones: Samara LPDS (Prosvet) and Sovkhoznaya-2 (Progress, Samara Obl.) and Zenzevatka (Volgograd Obl.) hit 19-20 and 24 Sep 2025, halting pumping; Volgograd Obl. pipeline hub feeding this route struck again 8 Jun 2026. Line itself restored/operational between strikes; stations damaged. Geometry: 33-vertex simplification of Global Energy Monitor route polyline (86 pts).

objects.name: Semikolodezyanska Oil Depot (Crimea)
objects.notes: Fuel transshipment hub supplying Russian occupation forces (diesel/fuel-oil/bitumen). coords approximate (city/district-level).

objects.name: Sever Products Pipeline (Kstovo-Yaroslavl-Kirishi-Primorsk)
objects.notes: Diesel (EN-590) export pipeline from Kstovo via Yaroslavl and Kirishi refineries to Primorsk port; expanded to ~25 Mt/yr by 2020 under Project Sever. Main pipeline outlet for Russian diesel exports via Baltic tankers. Simplified route, shares corridor with BPS-1 west of Yaroslavl.

objects.name: Yug Products Pipeline (Volgograd-Tikhoretsk-Novorossiysk)
objects.notes: Diesel pipeline Volgograd Refinery-Tinguta-Tikhoretsk-Novorossiysk, commissioned Apr 2018; up to ~6 Mt/yr of diesel to the Black Sea coast (Sheskharis/Novorossiysk export). Simplified route via Kotelnikovo-Zimovniki-Salsk corridor.

objects.name: ZapSibNeftekhim
objects.notes: World-class $9.5 billion integrated ethylene/polyethylene/polypropylene complex; 1.5 Mt/yr PE capacity, four polymerisation units. No confirmed drone strikes found in open sources - facility is ~2,000+ km from Ukraine border and has not been targeted in the verified strike window.
